problem.title: Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List
problem.description: Given the root of a binary tree, flatten the tree into a 'linked list'.

example.explanation: Pre-order traversal: 1,2,3,4,5,6. Chain via right pointers, set all left to null.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Morris Traversal [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: This approach flattens the tree in-place using a Morris-traversal-like technique without extra space. For each node with a left child, it finds the rightmost node of the left subtree and redirects it to point to the current node's right child, then moves the left subtree to the right. The key insight is that the rightmost node of the left subtree is always the predecessor of the current node's right subtree in preorder.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Recursive Right-First Postorder [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use a reverse-postorder traversal (right subtree first, then left). Keep a `prev` pointer (or instance variable) that tracks the previously visited node. For each node: recursively flatten the right subtree, then the left subtree. Set node.right = prev, node.left = null, and update prev = node. This threads the tree into a linked list in correct preorder. O(n) time O(n) recursion stack space.
